title: Sustainability & ESG in Projects
summary: For the 2026 exam and PMBOK 8, sustainability and ESG are built into the plan, not bolted on. They run through the Uncertainty, Delivery and Stakeholders performance domains and the Stewardship principle - appearing as a requirement, a quality-prevention choice, a procurement criterion, a compliance category, a risk source, and a value outcome to measure.

sections.heading: Sustainability is on the exam
sections.body: Sustainability is the second trend PMI named in the 2026 update, and it is deliberately spread across the outline rather than isolated. In PMBOK 8 it maps directly onto the Stewardship principle (be a diligent, caring steward) and threads through the Uncertainty, Delivery and Stakeholders performance domains. The expected mindset: sustainability is a real project requirement to plan for and measure, on par with scope, schedule and cost - built into the plan, not bolted on afterwards.

blocks.title: Answer signal
lines: If an option treats sustainability as optional or 'later', it is usually the weaker answer.
Where it shows up: a critical information requirement in planning, part of cost of quality, a compliance category, and a source of risk.
PMBOK 8 lens: Stewardship principle + Uncertainty / Delivery / Stakeholders domains.

sections.heading: The triple bottom line
sections.body: Sustainable project thinking balances three kinds of value. A strong project manager looks for solutions that protect all three rather than trading social or environmental harm for short-term cost or speed.

lines: Communities and workers.
Health and safety.
Fairness and social impact.
Emissions and waste.
Energy use.
Resource use and reuse.
The traditional financial result.
Long-term viability, not just short-term cost or speed.

blocks.title: Exam tip
blocks.text: When choices pit cost or schedule against environmental or social harm, look for the option that integrates all three rather than sacrificing one.

sections.heading: Sustainability as a requirement and constraint
sections.body: Treat sustainability requirements like any other. Because it is a requirement, sustainability is also subject to change control - you don't quietly drop it under schedule pressure. In PMBOK 8 this lives in the Stakeholders and Delivery domains: capture the need, define it, and reflect it in how you deliver value.

items.title: Capture with stakeholders
lines: Elicit sustainability needs as critical information requirements during planning.

items.title: Write it into scope
lines: Reflect it in scope and acceptance criteria - how you define 'done'.

items.title: Reflect it in the plan
lines: Fold it into the integrated plan and baselines so it flows into design, supplier and delivery decisions.

items.title: Protect it under change control
lines: It is a requirement, so it goes through change control - don't silently drop it under schedule pressure.

blocks.title: Circular economy
lines: Design outputs to be durable, reusable, repairable and recyclable.
Design out waste from the start rather than managing it at the end.
This is a design decision, so it belongs in scope and acceptance criteria - retrofitting it late is costly and risky.

sections.heading: Sustainability, quality and cost of quality
sections.body: The 2026 outline pairs sustainability with cost of quality, and PMBOK 8's Quality principle says build quality in rather than inspect it in. Designing for sustainability - less waste, less rework, durable and efficient outputs - is a prevention cost that avoids larger failure costs later. Sustainable choices and quality choices usually reinforce each other rather than compete.

rows.value: Design out waste and rework; durable, efficient outputs. A bit more up front.
Waste, rework, and remediation downstream - the larger cost.

blocks.title: Exam tip
blocks.text: Frame sustainability as prevention: a small investment up front avoids waste, rework and remediation later - the same logic as cost of quality.

sections.heading: Procurement and ESG
sections.body: Much of a project's environmental and social footprint sits in its supply chain. Sustainable procurement pushes ESG (Environmental, Social, Governance) criteria out beyond your own team's work. Vendor sustainability performance becomes part of how you evaluate and manage suppliers - part of the Project Work and Delivery domains.

items.text: Environmental - emissions, waste, energy, resource use.
Social - labour practices, health and safety, communities.

items.title: Set ESG selection criteria
lines: Put sustainability into source-selection criteria, not just price and capability.

items.title: Choose responsible suppliers
lines: Weigh vendor ESG performance in the award decision.

items.title: Write it into contracts
lines: Make sustainability obligations contractual terms, not aspirations.

items.title: Verify performance
lines: Confirm suppliers actually meet the obligations; treat it as part of vendor management.

blocks.title: Exam tip
blocks.text: Put sustainability into source-selection criteria and contract terms, not just into your own team's work.

sections.heading: Sustainability as compliance and risk
sections.body: Sustainability is now a compliance category alongside security and health and safety, and a genuine source of risk - the heart of the Uncertainty performance domain. The PM confirms the requirements, classifies them, weighs the consequences, and acts to stay compliant, while identifying and responding to sustainability threats.

items.title: Confirm requirements
lines: Identify relevant regulations and ESG-reporting obligations early.

items.title: Classify them
lines: Sort by category and applicability.

items.title: Weigh non-compliance
lines: Consider legal, financial and reputational consequences.

items.title: Act to stay compliant
lines: Build the controls in and track them through delivery.
Environmental events.
Regulatory change.
Reputational exposure.
Identify, analyse, and plan responses.
Communicate when a risk becomes an issue.

blocks.title: Exam tip
blocks.text: Missing a sustainability regulation is a compliance failure with legal and reputational fallout - confirm requirements early and track them.

sections.heading: Measuring sustainable value
sections.body: Value in the 2026 exam and PMBOK 8 includes sustainability outcomes, not just financial return - a Measurement and Delivery domain concern tied to the Value principle. Verify a measurement system is in place to track the sustainability benefits you promised, and review them through delivery and into benefits realization after handover.

rows.value: Emissions avoided, waste reduced, energy saved.
Social impact delivered to communities and workers.
Through delivery and into benefits realization after handover.

blocks.title: Exam tip
blocks.text: If you committed to a sustainability outcome, you need a metric for it - unmeasured benefits don't count.

source: Aligned with the PMP Examination Content Outline - July 2026 (sustainability across planning, quality, compliance, and risk) and the PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition (Stewardship principle; Uncertainty, Delivery and Stakeholders performance domains).
